current_slice: Feature 069 portable consumer contract kits v1

feature_findings.finding: A manifest could silently remove a supported interaction.
feature_findings.resolution: Consumer-specific exact interaction inventories are enforced.

feature_findings.finding: ProcedureQuery interactions shared an ambiguous request example.
feature_findings.resolution: Each output has a canonical example and requested_output discriminator validation.

feature_findings.finding: Consumer-owned fields could be introduced only in a response schema.
feature_findings.resolution: Forbidden fields are recursively checked in schemas and examples, with non-removable baseline guards.

feature_findings.finding: Response correlation headers were not part of the manifest gate.
feature_findings.resolution: Every success/error response must advertise X-Request-Id.

feature_findings.finding: Kit path input could address files outside the allowlist.
feature_findings.resolution: Only the two canonical kit filenames are accepted before filesystem access.

feature_findings.finding: Documentation implied the provider CLI was directly portable.
feature_findings.resolution: Schemas/examples/manifests are portable; consumer repositories must run equivalent local verification.

open_findings.finding: OS Electoral and Content Agency have not executed the kits in their own repositories.
No authorized durable corpus bytes or real ingestion exist.
No approved human identity/session architecture or deterministic staging environment exists for E2E.
No production platform, observability, load/HA, recovery or privacy operations exist.

historical_findings.description: Schema-valid replay could alter authority or artifact state.
historical_findings.repair: Require response hash, identity, persisted aggregate lookup and exact canonical reconstruction; commit cleanup before emitting replay_invalid.

historical_findings.description: Public URL fields could persist embedded credentials or temporary signatures.
historical_findings.repair: Reject userinfo and credential/signature query parameters in HTTP and PostgreSQL constraints.

historical_findings.description: SELECT-star lateral queries required private columns despite minimum grants.
historical_findings.repair: Use explicit projections and column-level grants for artifact, job, procedure and version state.

historical_findings.description: Legacy document status defaulted to unknown for new catalog registrations.
historical_findings.repair: Set migration default to draft and derive official_source/status in a server-owned trigger.

historical_findings.description: Repeated rate denials could reuse one audit UUID and degrade to 500.
historical_findings.repair: Persist at most one blocked audit per window inside the rate transaction and return subsequent 429s without duplicate inserts.

historical_findings.description: PostgreSQL idempotency claim inferred new ownership from timestamp equality.
historical_findings.repair: Use INSERT rowCount as the only new-claim signal.

historical_findings.description: Transitive fast-uri 3.1.3 triggered a high advisory.
historical_findings.repair: Update lockfile-only resolution to fast-uri 3.1.4 and run clean npm ci plus all/prod audits.

historical_findings.finding: External query embedding initially executed inside the tenant PostgreSQL transaction.
historical_findings.resolution: prepareSearchCapability now executes before opening retrieval transactions; semantic replay is resolved before provider invocation.

historical_findings.finding: HTTP query embedding transport had no bounded timeout.
historical_findings.resolution: AbortController timeout defaults to 10 seconds and is bounded/configurable with QUERY_EMBEDDING_TIMEOUT_MS.

historical_findings.finding: Hash-valid replay could have preserved semantically corrupted source/citation/claim relationships.
historical_findings.resolution: Replay validates deterministic identity, source/citation linkage, exact excerpt claims, support authority and contradiction references; corrupt completed state is deleted in a committed transaction.

historical_findings.finding: ILIKE phrase search treated percent and underscore as wildcards.
historical_findings.resolution: Phrase search now uses literal case-insensitive strpos and smoke proves percent returns zero.

historical_findings.finding: Signed or credential-bearing URLs could enter response projections.
historical_findings.resolution: Database constraints plus runtime URL filtering reject credentials, signature/token parameters and overlong URLs.

historical_findings.finding: Duplicate citation identities and negative cosine values could violate response assumptions.
historical_findings.resolution: Candidates dedupe by version/section identity and similarity is clamped to zero with explicit score semantics.

historical_findings.finding: Broad artifact/scanner grants could leak private coordinates.
historical_findings.resolution: Runtime gate grants only required columns and asserts object/scanner/lease/pipeline fields remain unreadable.

historical_findings.finding: Comparative evidence must never be promoted to an ordinary supported claim.
historical_findings.resolution: Bundle mapper promotes only supported exact excerpts; comparative evidence remains citation/gap and replay rejects promotion.
